specific_mechanism: Ostwald ripening: in a solution containing particles of varying size, larger particles grow at the expense of smaller ones because smaller particles have higher chemical potential due to Gibbs-Thomson effect. Small particles dissolve and redeposit on larger ones. Lifshitz-Slyozov-Wagner (LSW) theory predicts self-similar particle-size distribution with characteristic scaling.
llm_application: Model agent memory library coarsening as Ostwald ripening: small/specialized memory items are absorbed into large/established items via repeated retrieval and consolidation. Predict the coarsening rate from retrieval-frequency × embedding-proximity. Use LSW-style self-similar distribution analysis to characterize the equilibrium memory-size distribution.
novelty_claim: Multi-tier LLM memory consolidation is heavily studied (TiMem 2601.02845 hierarchical consolidation; Lightweight Agent Memory 2604.07798 with dynamic pruning that 'evicts stale low-utility items and merges/compresses redundant content'; Memoria 2512.12686; Multiple Memory Systems 2508.15294). The Ostwald-ripening framing with Gibbs-Thomson Gibbs-rate prediction isn't directly imported but the underlying dynamic is the published consolidation mechanism.
